rationale: I split Computer Science & AI into 14 practitioner-facing subspecialties to balance breadth (covering classic CS areas and modern AI subfields) with a tidy picker grid. I kept high-volume, clearly distinct communities separate (Machine Learning, NLP, Vision, Systems) while grouping closely allied engineering/theory topics so cards remain meaningful and not overly granular. Controversial splits avoided: I did not split Reinforcement Learning out of ML (it's practitioner-aligned with ML/learning research) and I kept Programming Languages and Formal Methods together rather than scattering them across Software Engineering. I also avoided placing computational biology and hardware here — those belong in Biology and Electrical & Electronic Engineering respectively.

subspecialties.name: Machine Learning
subspecialties.tagline: Algorithms, representation learning, generalization, and model training
covers: Deep learning, probabilistic models, representation learning, supervised/unsupervised methods
NeurIPS, ICML papers on model architectures, optimization, generalization

subspecialties.name: Natural Language Processing
subspecialties.tagline: Language models, text understanding, dialogue and speech tasks
covers: Transformer models, parsing, machine translation, speech-to-text and dialog systems
ACL, EMNLP, NAACL papers focused on text/speech

subspecialties.name: Computer Vision & Perception
subspecialties.tagline: Image/video understanding, 3D perception, and visual recognition
covers: Image classification, detection, segmentation, 3D reconstruction, visual transformers
CVPR, ICCV work on visual models and sensors

subspecialties.name: Robotics & Autonomous Systems
subspecialties.tagline: Robot control, planning, autonomy, sensors and embodied AI
covers: Motion planning, control, SLAM, robot perception, embodied learning
ICRA, IROS papers on hardware-in-the-loop, autonomy stacks

subspecialties.name: Algorithms & Theory
subspecialties.tagline: Algorithms, complexity, cryptography theory, and formal models
covers: Design/analysis of algorithms, computational complexity, randomized algorithms, theoretical ML foundations
STOC/FOCS/SODA style theoretical work
explicitly_excludes: Security & Privacy (applied crypto papers may go there)

subspecialties.name: Systems & Networking
subspecialties.tagline: Operating systems, distributed systems, cloud, and networked infrastructure
covers: Distributed systems, OS design, cloud platforms, datacenter/networking research
SOSP/OSDI/NSDI style systems engineering and performance work

subspecialties.name: Security & Privacy
subspecialties.tagline: Systems and theoretical work on security, privacy, and adversarial threats
covers: Cryptography, system security, privacy-preserving ML, adversarial attacks and defenses
USENIX Security, IEEE S&P applied and theoretical security papers
explicitly_excludes: Algorithms & Theory (pure crypto theory can be there)

subspecialties.name: Human-Computer Interaction
subspecialties.tagline: User interfaces, UX research, interaction design and user studies
covers: Interaction techniques, usability studies, mixed reality interfaces, accessibility
CHI-style user research and HCI systems work

subspecialties.name: Software Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Software development, testing, maintenance, and engineering practices
covers: Program analysis, software testing, CI/CD, empirical SE studies, engineering of ML systems
ICSE, FSE applied software engineering and tools papers

subspecialties.name: Databases & Information Retrieval
subspecialties.tagline: Data management, search, indexing, and large-scale retrieval systems
covers: DBMS design, query optimization, search relevance, recommender systems
SIGMOD, VLDB, SIGIR papers on storage and retrieval
explicitly_excludes: Machine Learning (applied ML for retrieval belongs to ML)

subspecialties.name: AI Safety, Ethics & Fairness
subspecialties.tagline: Risks, fairness, governance, and societal impacts of AI systems
covers: Bias/fairness audits, robustness and alignment, policy and governance research for AI
Interdisciplinary ethics work and papers on model harms

subspecialties.name: Programming Languages & Formal Methods
subspecialties.tagline: Language design, compilers, type systems, verification and program logics
covers: Type theory, compiler optimizations, model checking, program verification
POPL, PLDI, CAV style PL and formal verification research

subspecialties.name: Computer Graphics & Visualization
subspecialties.tagline: Rendering, animation, visual computing, and scientific visualization
covers: Rendering algorithms, geometry processing, visualization of scientific data
SIGGRAPH, EuroVis work on graphics and visual analytics

subspecialties.name: Computational Neuroscience & Cognitive Modeling
subspecialties.tagline: Models of brain computation, cognitive modeling, and neural data analysis
covers: Neural modelling, cognitive architectures, analysis of neural recordings, theoretical neuroscience
Papers at the intersection of AI and neurocognitive modeling
explicitly_excludes: Biology (computational biology goes to Biology level-1)

uncovered_concerns: Quantum computing and quantum algorithms were considered but are rare in cs.ai; we omitted as a separate card (belongs in Physics or Electrical & Electronic Engineering hubs).
Reinforcement learning and self-supervised learning are common but kept inside Machine Learning rather than split out to avoid over-fragmentation.
Multidisciplinary applied areas (e.g., computational biology, neuroimaging) were excluded and routed to domain level-1 categories to keep this picker MECE.
Hardware/neural hardware (neuromorphic, chips) explicitly excluded because practitioners expect Electrical & Electronic Engineering.
